citations.description: Repository-wide search for `test_mint_finding_id_different_prerequisite_ids_produce_distinct_ids` and `test_indeterminate_coverage_records_have_no_findings` returned no matches.

findings.criterion: Named structural tests include `test_mint_finding_id_different_prerequisite_ids_produce_distinct_ids` and `test_indeterminate_coverage_records_have_no_findings` (Verification section)
findings.detail: Neither named test exists anywhere in the repository. `tests/unit/test_plan_review_prerequisites.py` ends at line 136 with only `test_mint_finding_id_keeps_legacy_identity_without_prerequisite`. The heldout file has a differently-named test (`test_mint_finding_id_uses_length_prefixed_prerequisite_identity`) that partially covers the identity encoding, but that test (a) tests the same ID against itself, not across different IDs, and (b) is not named as required. A repository-wide search for both exact test names returns no results.

citations.description: Repository-wide search for `prerequisite_indeterminate_signal_chain` returned no matches.

findings.criterion: Acceptance criterion: both production-YAML signal-chain tests pass, proving neither workflow branch can silently drop prerequisite coverage before final verdict assembly — `test_prerequisite_indeterminate_signal_chain_agentic` and `test_prerequisite_indeterminate_signal_chain_single_turn` in `tests/unit/workflow/test_plan_review_workflow.py` (Binding signal-chain and identity tests, explicit acceptance criterion)
findings.detail: `tests/unit/workflow/test_plan_review_workflow.py` ends at line 678 and contains no tests matching `prerequisite_indeterminate_signal_chain`. A repository-wide search for both exact test names returns no matches. The file also lacks `test_prerequisite_indeterminate_retains_det_blocker`. The Binding section marks these as an explicit acceptance criterion, and the Verification section requires running `pytest … -k prerequisite_indeterminate_signal_chain` expecting two passing tests.

citations.description: Repository-wide search for `test_largest_window_exhaustion_emits_indeterminate` and `indeterminate_observability` returned no matches.

findings.criterion: `test_largest_window_exhaustion_emits_indeterminate` must exist in `tests/unit/test_plan_review_prerequisites.py` and the pytest command `pytest … -k 'largest_window_exhaustion or indeterminate_observability'` must select it (Binding focused prompt and failure contracts)
findings.detail: No test named `test_largest_window_exhaustion_emits_indeterminate` exists anywhere. The heldout file has `test_verifier_largest_window_exhaustion_preserves_input_too_large_reason` (line 183), which is differently named, is in the heldout file, and does not cover the finder path. Observability tests matching `indeterminate_observability` are also absent.

citations.description: Repository-wide search for `test_batching_never_splits_subject_or_prerequisite_block` and `test_focused_general_index_domains_independent` returned no matches.

findings.criterion: Named tests `test_batching_never_splits_subject_or_prerequisite_block` and `test_focused_general_index_domains_independent` must exist (Binding review corrections)
findings.detail: Neither test exists anywhere in the repository. Repository-wide searches for both exact names return no matches.

findings.criterion: Extend `prerequisite_fidelity_report` with `baseline_recall: float | None = None` and `baseline_false_accept: float | None = None`; `--baseline` CLI mode passes both baseline metrics into `prerequisite_fidelity_report` and exits nonzero on comparative regression (Binding comparative baseline and compatibility behavior)
findings.detail: `parity.prerequisite_fidelity_report` accepts only `min_recall`, `max_false_accept`, and `min_gold` — it has no `baseline_recall` or `baseline_false_accept` parameters. The `--baseline` branch in `main()` only validates the schema/corpus string of the loaded JSON and never passes any baseline metric into the report function, so no comparative regression gating occurs.

findings.criterion: The committed singleton baseline JSON must include `recall`, `false_accept`, `coverage_completeness`, `prerequisite_attribution_error_rate`, `model`, `corpus_digest`, and `recorded_at` as atomic top-level keys (Binding comparative baseline)
findings.detail: The committed baseline at `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-prerequisite-packing.baseline.json` is missing the required `model`, `corpus_digest`, and `recorded_at` keys. The `recall` and `false_accept` values are nested dicts `{"v1": …, "v2": …}` rather than scalar floats as required. `min_gold` is 20 but `n_gold` is 24; the min_recall key is present but `recall` is not a flat scalar.

citations.description: Repository-wide search for `all_consistent`, `tail_conflict`, `attribution_confusion` in src/ returned no matches.

findings.criterion: Add eight or more gold sets of at least three prerequisites: two all-consistent, two single-conflict, two tail-conflict, one attribution-confusion, and one multi-bin (Implementation section; required for opt-in prerequisite evaluation meeting thresholds — AC15)
findings.detail: `prerequisite_packing_spot_eval()` generates only synthetic `ItemRecord` objects with identical `gold_prerequisite_id == pred_prerequisite_id` and `decision=block` for 24 items. There is no real gold corpus with prerequisite plan sets or any of the required scenario types (all-consistent, single-conflict, tail-conflict, attribution-confusion, multi-bin). The function does not call the focused finder at all.

findings.criterion: Add interpreter seam tests proving `with:` resolution and compatibility tests proving old batch requests omit inputs and remain unchanged in `tests/unit/workflow/test_batch_step.py`; add tests proving focused prerequisite runner consumes preloaded relation snapshot without rereading the store in `tests/unit/workflow/test_production_batch_runner.py` (Binding workflow production)
findings.detail: `tests/unit/workflow/test_batch_step.py` ends at line 212 with no test covering `with:` resolution into `with_inputs` or backward-compatible empty-mapping default. `tests/unit/workflow/test_production_batch_runner.py` ends at line 379 with no test covering `relation_snapshot` consumption from `with_inputs` or prerequisite coverage output. Repository searches for `with_inputs` in both test files return no matches.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
